dev_directories.name: EntrepreneurOS
dev_directories.unique: Most architecturally complex. AI gateway with 5 providers. Clerk auth. CRM + org chart + task board + workflows.

dev_directories.name: CreatorOS
dev_directories.unique: Social creator platform. Passport auth. Posts/marketplace/communities. SendGrid email. Simplest.

dev_directories.name: LyfeOS
dev_directories.unique: Most feature-rich. Life gamification. Quests/missions/rituals. Stripe billing. Firebase + Passport dual auth. 7 migrations.

dev_directories.name: OS
dev_directories.notes: Full OS repo mirror. Behind VPS by several commits. Post-convergence structure.

dev_directories.name: OS (Projects)
dev_directories.notes: STALE copy — pre-convergence era structure. Should be considered dead.

eos.schema_divergence: Monolith Replit-era schema vs VPS UMH-separated schema

comparison_with_vps_saas.notes: Beast EOS is Replit-era monolith (Clerk auth, all-in-one schema). VPS saas/ is post-architecture-separation (UMH auth, split platform/projection tables). Same domain, divergent implementation.

summary: Beast /dev has 3 full Trinity apps (EOS=603 files, CreatorOS=272, LyfeOS=854) + 2 OS copies + 5 empty dirs. All apps share Replit-origin stack but differ in auth (Clerk/Passport/Firebase), AI providers, and domain. EOS Beast version has DIVERGED from VPS saas/ — different auth system (Clerk vs UMH), different schema structure (monolith vs separated). LyfeOS is largest by files, EOS is most complex architecturally.
